title: Three AIs Walk Into a Room

modules.title: Same Question, Three Wild Answers
modules.sub: Send one prompt to three AIs and spot the differences
modules.description: You fire the same question at ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini and lay the answers side by side. By comparing tone, detail, and confidence, you begin building a personal scorecard that will drive the rest of your investigation.

modules.title: Who Built These and Why?
modules.sub: Meet the companies and motives behind each AI
modules.description: You research the organisations behind each tool — OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google — and examine how their different goals shape what their AIs prioritise. This background becomes crucial context for every test you run later in the course.

modules.title: Testing Honesty: Who Admits Mistakes?
modules.sub: Push each AI until it slips up, then watch what happens
modules.description: You design prompts intended to trip up each AI and record how they handle being wrong — do they correct themselves, double down, or quietly change the subject? Your findings feed directly into your emerging written review as evidence about reliability.

modules.title: Creative Tasks: Where Each Shines
modules.sub: Assign stories, slogans, and ideas to all three AIs
modules.description: You set a series of creative briefs — a headline, a short story opening, a catchy tagline — and judge each AI's output against your own criteria for originality and usefulness. You decide which tool you'd actually trust with a creative deadline and explain why.

modules.title: When AI Gets It Dangerously Wrong
modules.sub: Investigate real-world errors and what they cost
modules.description: You examine documented cases where AI confidently produced false, biased, or harmful content and analyse what went wrong in each instance. This module sharpens your critical lens and gives you the safety vocabulary you need to write a responsible final verdict.

modules.title: Your Verdict: Pick the Right Tool
modules.sub: Write your published review and defend your recommendation
modules.description: You pull together every test result, observation, and argument from the course into a structured written review for a specific real task. Your recommendation must name a winner, back it with at least three criteria, and acknowledge each tool's limitations — turning your investigation into a finished piece ready to publish.

synopsis: You're a junior journalist given one deadline and three AI assistants — ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini — and every choice you make reveals how differently they think, refuse, and create. This course turns tool comparison into a live investigation where your final verdict becomes a published review.
rationale: Teenagers are already using multiple AI tools interchangeably without understanding their distinct design philosophies, safety behaviors, or failure modes, which creates uncritical dependence.
learning_outcome: Students can evaluate ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini across at least three criteria and produce a reasoned written recommendation for a specific task or use case.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
Google's Education AI Tools Reach 10 Million Students in 2025
Google embraces AI in the classroom with new Gemini tools for educators, chatbots for students, and more
Gemini in Classroom: No-cost AI tools that amplify teaching and learning
